reviews.review_text: This place was amazing for the first time being here and the reviews saying there are thieves and stuff, we had no encounter with those either. Being next to the river was 👍 and the canoes were clean from the hire. The wildlife was amazing as I got to see some beautiful birds. The option of river and ocean was amazing the river was shallow at low and high tide, the beach was amazing nearby. Although I think 3 nights was enough to enjoy this amazing experience

Extremely poor handling of a delicate situation. Had a family emergency and 2/4 attendees had to cancel booking. I have already paid the full amount and they would not refund a cent back. Crazy how a multimillion dollar company cant hand some cash back 2 weeks before a booking in a time of need. Such a scam.
Shower and toilet were very  clean great location.  But had my car vandalised.
Caravan park
Nice parks clean amenities but getting very pricey for a holiday  unfortunately especially when reflections is State Government owned !!!

We loved staying in this park. During the week out of school holidays and in the cooler months, it’s quiet. A short walk to the beach and town centre, this park is great for vans and caravans. We’ll be back.

The issue with staying in the cabins is people just walk in off the street and set up for the day in front of your cabin.. just walking in off the street and being rude when asked to move.. put some gates or a fence around the camp grounds so paying customers dont have to deal with it..
Great place and quiet location 👌
Nice park but way overpriced and the noise from the trucks is bad

Nice office staff.
Not on beach but on a nice creek.
A quiet accomodation park close to town, with access close to the creek, river and beach. Amenities in excellent order and clean.

Our 3rd year back… while site slab may need some upgrading, it’s still a great place for the family.
A park would be great, but possibly the one at the entrance is being upgraded due to safety reasons.

Our eskies were stolen from the campsite overnight so make sure you secure your stuff. Police and locals that live on the campsite permanently said it happens quite a bit - disappointed the camp reception didn’t mention anything to us as it’s obviously a common issue. Not great experience knowing people were stealing stuff from our camp while we were asleep. The second night someone else up on a different site to us also had all their alcohol stolen , so definitely not a one off.

The campsite itself is nice and in a good location. However, on our very first night both of our eskies were stolen. After speaking with a few permanent residents, we learned that theft (of eskies, drinks, bikes, etc.) happens here quite often if items are left outside. For a  more pricey camping experience, you’d expect the park to take more care in warning guests or putting measures in place to prevent this. Instead, the lady at reception brushed it off as our fault, which was pretty disappointing. On top of that, you’re not allowed to have fire pits, which takes away from the camping atmosphere. A shout out to Shaun (I think that was his name) who works there - he was really helpful and drove around to assist us in looking for our things.

The place itself is lovely, however the thing that let it down for us was that there is a known problem of looting of sites and we were not previously notified. The staff said it’s only a problem in school holidays, however we came during school time and still had two eskies stolen. Other residents and locals, including the police, said this happens very frequently.
Great place nice lady working reception

Place is really nice for camping, they have hot water, kitchen and bathroom for families with a little bathtub for babies that was really handy for me.
The only negative thing was that you can't have a firepit close to your tent or do a barbecue there as well. Only at specific places around the camping
Overall was really nice
Place was lovely , clean and tidy. Only issue was the highway traffic, trucks and compression brakes. Didn't sleep a wink
Right on the water. Clean ammenities, walk across the road to town and the Brunswick Hotel. Walk to beach.

Have stayed here a number of times but have to say this was our least satisfactory visit. Apart from the receptionist, the rest of the staff were particularly unfriendly - to the point of totally ignoring our greetings. The caravan site we had by the riverside was $72 a night but don't feel this price was warranted. There was no concrete slab provided and despite most other sites without slabs being fitted with a permanent groundsheet to set up on, we instead had dirty gravel. The grey water waste point hole for our site was barely below the height of our van's waste exit so our sink would barely drain. At night there were no lights at all between our van and the amenities block. The people in the long term accommodation  near us were constantly smoking marijuana on their front deck,  making a haze of strong smelling smoke that would be pretty awful for a family to stay next to. Amenities were clean but pretty unimpressive compared to $35 a night places we've stayed at recently. Location is handy to town but with the number of shops now empty there's not much to keep you occupied. The $5 an hour or $25 a day parking fee certainly makes you glad that you can leave your car in the caravan park. Bruns sure isn't the place it used to be.🥹

Very disappointed with management.
Due to arrive Thursday 22/5/25 for a 3 night stay on a caravan powered site. Due to northern nsw flooding we are trapped at Crescent Head.
We have already paid in full, no refund was offered, offered only to use as credit until end of June 25.

Dont pay in full at this park before you arrive is my take from this experience

We were looking forward to this area as not been there before.

Great location, walking distance to cafes, restaurants, pub and bakery.
Clean toilets and shower blocks
We had the spot by the creek. Beautiful view of the sunrise. Toilets and showers are clean. They regularly clean the bbq picnic area too.
Overpriced campsites because Reflections have the monopoly in Brunswick heads. (All three parks!) Great location and check in time is 11am which is great.

Disappointing concrete pad covered in bird poo, Car got poo’d on and lady at reception was not inclined to refund after we decided to leave after one night.
Pretty shameful hosts, just a big greedy chain of caravan parks up the east coast.
1 star.
Beautiful scenery and great amenities ~ very clean! ~ only bummer was we had a spot with no shade, it was so hot 🥵 Hot tip ~ ask for a shady spot x

What an amazing place. Clean cabin with new furniture and appliances. Really comfortable beds. I rate the shower and water pressure a 10 and best I’ve had. Everything in walking distance . Becca, Greg and Shane bent over backwards to help us. Great staff.Location is amazing with 5 min walk to beach and multiple entries to swim in simpsons creek safely.  No pool but that just adds to the serenity and just float on a tyre in the creek. 10 outta 10. Love ya work. Dan Robertson, Brisbane

I have friends staying at reflections Brunswick every year right on the river the park is lovely toilet /bathroom facilities are clean n looked after well ..
park is maintained  gardens and  clean n tidy good place to stay n walk to the shops close by highly recommend 💯
Very friendly and helpful staff,had two issues and they helped immediately. Thankyou to the Groundsman for helping us fix the leak in our van in bad weather.
Loved our mini getaway. Huge shoutout to the cleaners. Our cabin was spotless, immaculate. Fresh and bright. Wonderful! We loved our stay!
Not allowing people to charge their E.V. Avoid!

We stayed for a long weekend in the cabins and could not fault our stay. Perfect location with everything in walking distance (no need to drive anywhere), clean cabins and grounds, and great check in with Lorna! Park is great for all ages and the option to swim in the creek or beach is great for those with kids. Although it was only a long weekend stay, we felt we really got to switch off and relax. Looking forward to returning again.
Best place you could possibly stay in this area. The managers bent over backwards for us. Could not recommend more highly will definitely be back

We booked caravan site in school holidays at $90 per night, and could not get there on the dates we booked, we tried to cancel, we couldn't get our money back, because of peak season, 11 days notice?? We gave. For such a bizzy park that site would of been booked again in 20 min and no offer of a credit for another time. And asked us for evidence of why we couldn't make it !!! Very 😞
Big shout out to the staff of  Brunswick Heads hoilday  Park .Thankyou you for the excellent  service & to Tanya for  the such clean amenities. What fabulous location.
Super close to the town and footbridge, short walk to the beach, IGA and BWS all walking distance. Family friendly nice staff.
Too much traffic noise.Would not stay again
Lovely stay. Coming back next year.
Could not fault this place. Stayed in the superior cabin for the wife's birthday. The weather and helped, assisted by the beautiful turquoise estuary. Will certainly be back.

Second year staying here in our van. Love the park. Close to everything without the noise. Monthly market close, canoe hire, shops, restaurants galore etc. Amenities cleaned or checked several times a day. Always smell good and never a wait for shower or toilet. I love to paint around there which plenty within walking distance, including the beach and the biggest trap - the ice-cream shop! Staff have always been friendly as were the park residents. Bush turkeys love the bins though so keep anything plastic inside. They pull the plastic like it’s a pokey machine hoping to score scraps. Lots of bird life and beautiful sunsets. We donate videos each year for visitors to borrow if they have a player too.

Asked for a riverfront room for my girlfriend's birthday, was told with a phone call that this was confirmed, but upon arrival we were instead given a room with a lovely view of the carpark.
Provided this feedback and no response given.
Will never stay here again.
A really nice and peaceful park. Amenities are really clean. Will definitely be returning
Best Location, Fishing, swimming, surfing. BEST Pub, great entertainment, fantastic
Wonderful stay, basically in town and a quick walk to the beach. River at the door step. Clean facilities and good showers.
Great place to stay with kids. Walking distance to shops and beaches. Hire pedal carts in the park. Modern clean amenities.
Staff were so helpful . Thankyou so much to the staff . Great location , we had an awesome family holiday ❤️
Always one of my favourite places to camp.   I cannot stay away
This is in a good location,  but the pricing is shameful. Families can no longer have an affordable holiday at a camp ground with price gouging like this.  Shame on the operators.
Stayed here once, its very good with nice facilities. Only thing I am a bit sad about was the amazing bird aviary they used to have at the front of business. would always enjoy visiting the birds.

We where told they closed the back half of camping only to get there and they opened it. Out joining sites where divided by trees. The sites where smaller than quoted. Management would not deal with loud drunk campers. Was flashed multiple times. They threw glass bottles at wildlife broke locks in bathrooms. Management didn't seem to care at all.

Again, the staff at Reflections Massey Greene ensured our stay was faultless.
Our site was nicely mowed & maintained by Webby, top bloke. I enjoyed a few ginger beers with him, and  he always had time for a quick chat.
Thoroughly enjoyed the prize draws coordinated by Lorna. Her dedication & enthusiasm are priceless & we all enjoyed our time, rain hail, or shine.
Also must say a special mention of park manager Jarrod. Again, he's always busy  at this time of yr managing two parks, but has time for a quick chat & checking up on myself & Webby re the ginger beers!!
Park bathroom facilities were always very clean & cleaning times were always posted on the doors,  very helpful.
Another excellent activity coordinated by Lorna this year is the Westpac bowls fund-raiser, a great day, breakfast & then laughs on the green, made some new friends & surprised myself with some lucky bowling.
I am looking forward to our next visit to Reflections Massey Greene in April for a get-together with friends & then again in May re Mullum 2 Bruns Paddle, which is another opportunity of supporting both the Brunswick Surf Lifesaving Club & local Rescue Service.
Possibly Reflections could have a prize this coming Christmas for the best Christmas lights display in the Parks, just a thought.
Again, I can highly recommend a stay at either 3 Reflections Parks in Bruns, something for everyone.
Clean park on the water edge. Nice walk across the nearby footbridge to the ocean. Easy walk to shops, cafes, restaurants, and bakery. Pretty spot.

Park is in a beautiful location, right on the river. Amenities were okay. The older amenities weren’t great and we chose to use the newer block only. Has a family bathroom which is good when you have young children. Some midgies at dusk, so we ate inside our van. Overall, a nice place to visit for a short stay.

Booked a site for a camper trailer, a very expensive park at nearly $1300 for a powered site for only 15 nights but we were told it was a good park / nice location. The site we were given was a double concrete slab with a bitumen road on either side and the ammenities block right across from us, there was nowhere we could put in a peg, we couldn’t set up our camper. We were told there were no other sites available and as we had entered the park we would lose our money if we left now. They took 2 hours to not find us a site at any of their nearby parks then told us we couldn’t get a credit either (I’d located a suitable site at the park we usually stay at in Byron Bay). finally, they agreed to try and call their regional manager and another 1/2 hour later we were offered a credit. We found the whole experience quite traumatic, not what you want when going on a much anticipated holiday. Also, the sites are tiny and right on top of each other, patrons are crammed in like sardines. Although the staff helped us get a suitable outcome in the end, we found them unsympathetic to our plight, considering I’d told them when booking that I had a camper trailer not a caravan they didn’t seem to care that the site was completely unsuitable.
Very clean and well looked after holiday park!!
Great location in a great town. A very popular park, easy walk to everything. Sites on the smallish side, typical of most older coastal parks. The grounds are clean and well kept

Good location for boardwalk walk and Brunswick shops.
Amenities clean and park well looked after. Barbeques available but no full camp kitchen. Enjoyed our brief stay, thank you
Excellent caravan park experience. Had van site on river ,spacious. Staff friendly groundsman good for a laugh, amenities spotless , lovely walks in area and Brunswick Heads is such a friendly town.
Top location in a pretty spot near the river and beaches. We got a powered site right near the water. Brilliant facilities. Great sms communications. Chill place popular with  families.
Nice place but very pricey at $90 for power site long weekend
Great place for a break.
Perfect location to explore Brunswick Heads, everything you need right at your doorstep.
